summary: Corrected several fighter-related function signatures and call sites so object parameters are explicit instead of omitted or typed as s32. The PR specifically declared func_8006F238 as taking an HSD_GObj*, declared func_8016B238 as taking void, and changed func_8009A804 and ftcliffcommon's func_80081504 from s32-style arguments to HSD_GObj* arguments. Koopa and cliff-common call sites were updated to pass the current gobj into func_8006F238 and func_8009A804.
what_changed: In include/functions.h, changed func_8006F238 from an unspecified-parameter declaration, s32 func_8006F238(), to s32 func_8006F238(struct _HSD_GObj*).
In include/functions.h, changed func_8009A804 from void func_8009A804(s32) to void func_8009A804(struct _HSD_GObj*), addressing the PR note that it was underspecified.
In include/functions.h, changed func_8016B238 from s32 func_8016B238() to s32 func_8016B238(void), making the no-argument contract explicit.
In src/melee/ft/chara/ftkoopa.c, updated func_80133654 and func_80133690 so calls to func_8006F238 pass the local HSD_GObj* gobj argument.
In src/melee/ft/ftcliffcommon.c and src/melee/ft/ftcliffcommon.h, changed func_80081504 from taking s32 arg0 to taking HSD_GObj* gobj, then passed that gobj to func_8006F238 and func_8009A804.
smart_moves: The fix paired prototype changes with all visible call-site updates, avoiding a header-only correction that would leave mismatched usage in fighter code.
Using HSD_GObj* instead of s32 for object-bearing arguments made the dataflow clearer in ftkoopa and ftcliffcommon, where the same gobj is forwarded into fighter/common helper functions.
The PR distinguished between a function that truly takes no arguments, func_8016B238(void), and functions whose prior empty parameter list was missing a real HSD_GObj* parameter.
decomp_lessons: In this codebase, an old-style C declaration like func() can hide missing parameter information; when evidence shows the function consumes the current fighter object, prefer an explicit HSD_GObj* prototype and update callers.

Do not leave object pointers typed as integer placeholders such as s32 when the call chain passes fighter game objects. Replace placeholder scalar types with HSD_GObj* once call-site evidence supports it.
When refining signatures in include/functions.h, also update local module headers such as ftcliffcommon.h so declarations stay consistent across global and module-level interfaces.
A function that takes no parameters should be declared with void, not an empty parameter list, to avoid ambiguity during future matching and type checking.
naming_conventions: Use HSD_GObj* gobj for fighter object parameters in source/module headers, matching existing ftkoopa and ftcliffcommon local variable naming.
Global include/functions.h prototypes may spell the same type as struct _HSD_GObj* where that header uses forward-declared struct names.
Avoid generic names like arg0 after the parameter type is known; rename to gobj when it is an HSD_GObj*.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use call-site dataflow to infer missing parameters: func_8006F238 was changed to accept the same gobj that enclosing fighter callbacks already receive.

If a callee is forwarded the same object argument from a wrapper, as with func_80081504 forwarding gobj to func_8009A804, propagate the corrected pointer type through both wrapper and callee declarations.
For no-argument functions, use an explicit void parameter list to constrain the prototype and reduce future mismatch noise.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching func_8006F238 to confirm whether the HSD_GObj* signature was retained or further renamed.
Search for remaining declarations in include/functions.h that use empty parameter lists despite likely known arguments.
Search for fighter helper wrappers that still take s32 arg0 but forward it to HSD_GObj*-typed functions.
Check whether func_8009A804 later received a descriptive name or module-local declaration beyond include/functions.h.
